description: Comprehensive list of all contact models available in PFC 5.0, with PFC5-specific syntax, properties, methods, and notes on which models are PFC7+ only. Extracted from pfc5help/common/contactmodel/.

sections.heading: PFC5 Models vs PFC7+ Only Models
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 has 12 contact models (documented in pfc5help):
  linear, linearcbond, linearpbond, hertz, hysteretic,
  rrlinear, flatjoint, smoothjoint, burger,
  null, thermalnull, thermalpipe

PFC 6.0/7.0/9.0 added 8 more models NOT in PFC 5.0:
  arrlinear    — Adhesive Rolling Resistance Linear (6.0+)
  bilinear     — Bilinear Contact Model (7.0+)
  eepa         — Edinburgh-Elasto-Plastic-Adhesive (7.0+)
  fish         — User-defined FISH contact model (7.0+)
  jkr          — Johnson-Kendall-Roberts adhesive (7.0+)
  lineardipole — Linear Dipole / magnetic (7.0+)
  softbond     — Soft-Bond with post-peak softening (6.0+)
  springnetwork — Rigid Body Spring Network (7.0+)

If you try to use any of these 8 models in PFC 5.0, you will get
an 'Unknown model' error.

sections.heading: 1. linear — Linear Elastic-Frictional
sections.content:
DEFAULT BEHAVIOR: Linear elastic with Coulomb friction limit. No tension.

Properties (modifiable):
  kn      FLT  [0,∞) default=0  — Normal stiffness [force/length]
  ks      FLT  [0,∞) default=0  — Shear stiffness [force/length]
  fric    FLT  [0,∞) default=0  — Friction coefficient
  dp_nratio FLT [0,∞) default=0 — Normal critical damping ratio
  dp_sratio FLT [0,∞) default=0 — Shear critical damping ratio
  dp_mode  INT  {0,1,2} default=0 — Dashpot mode
  lin_mode INT  {0,1,2} default=0 — Linear force mode
  lin_force VEC — Linear force (modifiable for reset)

Properties (read-only):
  emod    FLT — Effective modulus (set via method)
  kratio  FLT — Normal-to-shear stiffness ratio
  dp_force VEC — Dashpot force

Methods:
  deformability emod <E> kratio <R>

PFC5 Examples:
  cmat default model linear method deformability emod 1e9 kratio 1.0
  cmat default model linear property fric 0.5 dp_nratio 0.2
  contact method deform emod 20e9 krat 2.5

sections.heading: 2. linearcbond — Linear Contact Bond Model
sections.content:
Like linear model, but with a point-bond that resists force only (no moment).

The bond is a vanishingly small cement joining the two pieces at the contact point.

Properties (modifiable, same as linear plus):
  cb_ten    FLT  [0,∞) default=0 — Contact bond tensile strength [force]
  cb_coh    FLT  [0,∞) default=0 — Contact bond cohesion [force]
  cb_fa     FLT  [0,90) default=0 — Contact bond friction angle [degrees]
  cb_shear  FLT  read-only — Computed shear strength at sliding
  cb_state  INT  {0,1,2,3} — Bond state: 0=unbonded,1=..., read-only

Methods:
  bond [gap <f1> [<f2>]]   — Bond contacts
  deformability emod <E> kratio <R>
  unbond [gap <f1> [<f2>]] — Unbond contacts

Callback events: bond_break entries(3)=mode {1,2}

Note: cb_ten and cb_coh are in units of FORCE (not stress), unlike
flatjoint's fj_ten/fj_coh which are in stress units.

PFC5 Example:
  cmat default model linearcbond property cb_ten 1e6 cb_coh 1e6

sections.heading: 3. linearpbond — Linear Parallel Bond Model
sections.content:
Like linear model, but with a parallel bond that resists both force and moment.
The bond has a finite cross-section (radius = R * pb_rmul) and can break in
tension or shear, forming an unbonded linear contact upon breakage.

Properties (modifiable, same as linear plus):
  pb_ten    FLT  [0,∞) default=0  — Parallel bond tensile strength [stress]
  pb_coh    FLT  [0,∞) default=0  — Parallel bond cohesion [stress]
  pb_fa     FLT  [0,90) default=0 — Parallel bond friction angle [degrees]
  pb_kn     FLT  [0,∞) default=0  — Bond normal stiffness [stress/disp.]
  pb_ks     FLT  [0,∞) default=0  — Bond shear stiffness [stress/disp.]
  pb_rmul   FLT  (0,∞) default=1.0 — Bond radius multiplier
  pb_radius FLT  read-only — Bond radius (= R * pb_rmul)

Properties (read-only):
  pb_emod    FLT — Bond effective modulus
  pb_kratio  FLT — Bond normal-to-shear stiffness ratio
  pb_shear   FLT — Bond shear strength at centroid
  pb_sigma   FLT — Bond normal stress at centroid
  pb_tau     FLT — Bond shear stress at centroid
  pb_state   INT  {0,1,2,3} — Bond state
  pb_force   VEC — Bond force
  pb_moment  VEC — Bond moment

Methods:
  bond [gap <f1> [<f2>]]       — Bond contacts
  deformability emod <E> kratio <R>  — Set linear stiffness
  pb_deformability emod <E> kratio <R>  — Set bond stiffness
  unbond [gap <f1> [<f2>]]     — Unbond contacts

Callback events: bond_break entries(3)=mode {1,2}

PFC5 Example (rocktest/parallel_bond.p3dat):
  contact model linearpbond range contact type ball-ball
  contact method bond gap -1e-3
  contact method deform emod 1.0e9 krat 1.0
  contact method pb_deform emod 1.0e9 krat 1.0
  contact property pb_ten 10e6 pb_coh 50e6 pb_fa 0.0
  contact property fric 0.577

Note: In the example files, 'pb_deform' is the short form of 'pb_deformability'
and 'krat' is the short form of 'kratio'. Both work.

sections.heading: 4. hertz — Hertz-Mindlin (Nonlinear Elastic)
sections.content:
Nonlinear elastic contact model based on Hertz-Mindlin theory.
Normal force: F_n ~ delta^1.5 (nonlinear). No tension.
Key difference from linear: stiffness depends on particle size and overlap.

Properties (modifiable):
  hertz_poiss FLT  [0,0.5) default=0.0 — Hertz Poisson's ratio
  hertz_shear FLT  [0,∞) default=0.0  — Hertz shear modulus [stress]
  fric        FLT  [0,∞) default=0.0  — Friction coefficient
  dp_nratio   FLT  — Normal damping ratio
  dp_sratio   FLT  — Shear damping ratio
  dp_mode     INT  — Dashpot mode
  lin_mode    INT  — Linear force mode

Properties (read-only):
  hertz_g    FLT  — Current shear modulus
  hertz_nu   FLT  — Current Poisson's ratio

PFC5 Example:
  contact model hertz
  contact property hertz_poiss 0.2 hertz_shear 5e9 fric 0.7

Note: Hertz model has NO deformability method (kn/ks are not directly
specified; they emerge from particle geometry and material constants).
Must use 'cmat default model hertz' BEFORE particle generation for
proper contact formation.

sections.heading: 5. hysteretic — Hysteretic Contact Model
sections.content:
Based on Hertz model with unloading/reloading hysteresis for energy dissipation
during impact. Same nonlinear F_n ~ delta^1.5 relationship, but with
hysteretic damping (different loading/unloading paths).

Properties (modifiable):
  hys_poiss  FLT  [0,0.5) — Poisson's ratio of the material
  hys_shear  FLT  [0,∞)   — Shear modulus of the material [stress]
  hys_damp   FLT  (0,1)   — Hysteretic damping fraction
  fric       FLT  — Friction coefficient
  dp_nratio  FLT  — Normal damping ratio
  dp_sratio  FLT  — Shear damping ratio
  dp_mode    INT  — Dashpot mode

Properties (read-only):
  hys_g      FLT  — Current shear modulus
  hys_nu     FLT  — Current Poisson's ratio

Note: No deformability method. Must be assigned before cycling.
Only use when impact/hysteretic damping is needed.
For standard BPM work, use 'linear' or 'flatjoint' instead.

sections.heading: 6. burger — Burger's Viscoelastic Model
sections.content:
Burger's model (Kelvin + Maxwell in series) for creep behavior.
Deformation is time-dependent under constant load.

Properties (modifiable):
  bur_kn_kel FLT  — Kelvin normal stiffness [stress/disp.]
  bur_ks_kel FLT  — Kelvin shear stiffness [stress/disp.]
  bur_vn_kel FLT  — Kelvin normal viscosity [stress*time/disp.]
  bur_vs_kel FLT  — Kelvin shear viscosity [stress*time/disp.]
  bur_kn_max FLT  — Maxwell normal stiffness [stress/disp.]
  bur_ks_max FLT  — Maxwell shear stiffness [stress/disp.]
  bur_vn_max FLT  — Maxwell normal viscosity [stress*time/disp.]
  bur_vs_max FLT  — Maxwell shear viscosity [stress*time/disp.]
  fric       FLT  — Friction coefficient
  dp_nratio  FLT  — Normal damping ratio
  dp_sratio  FLT  — Shear damping ratio
  dp_mode    INT  — Dashpot mode
  lin_mode   INT  — Force mode

Properties (read-only):
  bur_normal_strain  FLT — Normal strain
  bur_shear_strain   FLT — Shear strain magnitude
  bur_force_kelvin    VEC — Kelvin force
  bur_force_maxwell   VEC — Maxwell force

Note: Used for time-dependent simulations (creep, relaxation).
Not suitable for standard strength testing.

sections.heading: 7. rrlinear — Rolling Resistance Linear Model
sections.content:
Linear model with an additional rolling resistance torque that opposes
relative rolling motion. Simulates surface roughness effects at the
contact level without explicitly modeling angular particles.

Properties (modifiable, same as linear plus):
  rr_fric   FLT  [0,∞) default=0.0 — Rolling resistance coefficient
  rr_mode   INT  {0,1,2} default=0 — Rolling resistance mode
  rr_force  VEC  read-only — Rolling resistance moment

Methods:
  deformability emod <E> kratio <R>

Note: rr_fric is NOT a friction angle but a coefficient
(like rolling friction). Typical values: 0.05-0.3.
Use for granular materials where particle shape matters
(sand, gravel) without using clumps.

PFC5 Example:
  contact model rrlinear
  contact property fric 0.5 rr_fric 0.1
  contact method deform emod 1e9 krat 2.5

sections.heading: 8. flatjoint — Flat-Joint Model
sections.content:
See dedicated 'flatjoint.json' in pfc5-guide for full reference.

Summary: Finite-size interface discretized into elements.
Bonded elements: linear elastic until tensile/shear strength exceeded.
Unbonded elements: linear elastic + frictional with Coulomb slip.

Key properties: fj_ten, fj_coh, fj_fa, fj_fric, fj_kn, fj_ks, fj_rmul
Key methods: deformability emod <E> kratio <R>, bond gap <G>, unbond
Callback: bond_break entries(3)=mode {1=tension, 2=shear}

sections.heading: 9. smoothjoint — Smooth-Joint Model
sections.content:
Models planar interfaces (joints, bedding planes, faults) that allow
particles on either side to slide past each other without interlocking.
The contact orientation is fixed to the joint plane normal, not the
ball-ball line-of-centers.

Properties (modifiable):
  sj_kn      FLT  [0,∞) default=0  — Joint normal stiffness [stress/disp.]
  sj_ks      FLT  [0,∞) default=0  — Joint shear stiffness [stress/disp.]
  sj_fric    FLT  [0,∞) default=0  — Joint friction coefficient
  sj_ten     FLT  [0,∞) default=0  — Joint tensile strength [stress] (bonded)
  sj_coh     FLT  [0,∞) default=0  — Joint cohesion [stress] (bonded)
  sj_fa      FLT  [0,90) default=0 — Joint friction angle [degrees] (bonded)
  sj_dilation FLT [0,∞) default=0  — Joint dilation angle [degrees]
  sj_local   BOOL {true,false} default=false — Use local vs global joint orientation

Properties (read-only):
  sj_emod    FLT — Effective modulus
  sj_kratio  FLT — Normal-to-shear stiffness ratio
  sj_shear   FLT — Shear strength at bond break
  sj_sigma   FLT — Normal stress
  sj_gap     FLT — Joint gap
  sj_area    FLT — Joint area
  sj_normal  VEC  — Joint normal direction unit vector

Methods:
  deformability emod <E> kratio <R>
  bond [gap <f1> [<f2>]]
  unbond [gap <f1> [<f2>]]

Callback events: bond_break entries(3)=mode {1,2}

PFC5 Example (rocktest/smooth_joint.p3dat):
  ; Assign smoothjoint to contacts crossing a plane
  contact model smoothjoint ...
      range contact type ball-ball ...
      plane dip 45 dd 0 origin 0 0 0
  contact method deform emod 1e9 krat 1.0
  contact property sj_fric 0.577 sj_ten 0.0 sj_coh 0.0

sections.heading: 10. null — Null Model (PFC5 Default)
sections.content:
The Null model maintains a generalized internal force of zero (F_c = M_c = 0).
Newly created contacts default to Null in PFC5. No forces are transmitted.

CRITICAL: Always set 'cmat default model ...' before 'ball distribute'
if you want contacts to carry load. Otherwise, particles will pass
through each other with zero interaction.

No modifiable properties. No methods.

sections.heading: 11. thermalnull — Null Thermal Contact Model
sections.content:
Default thermal contact model. No heat conduction.
No power is generated. For thermal-mechanical analysis only.

No modifiable thermal properties.

sections.heading: 12. thermalpipe — Thermal Pipe Contact Model
sections.content:
Models 1D heat conduction pipe with thermal resistance per unit length.
For thermal-mechanical analysis only.

Properties (modifiable):
  th_eta   FLT  [0,∞) — Thermal resistance per unit length [temp/power]
